codeReview.summary: The commit correctly removes chart downsampling so exchange volume routes now expose persisted live refresh points directly, and the added app regressions cover unknown-exchange failures plus short-window and explicit-range ownership behavior aligned with the feature contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-provider-worker skill requires a failing characterization/regression test first, but this worker explicitly skipped an isolated red-first cycle and implemented tests plus code together. The procedure may need to acknowledge when tightly scoped route changes are reviewed from existing characterization instead of a separate failing-first command.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback marks followedProcedure=false for step 2, and worker-transcripts.jsonl for session 2cb5cc39-e01b-4e4c-85ee-e3ea4243bcce shows ApplyPatch edits landed before any successful targeted test run.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-provider-worker skill says to run the targeted validation set, but assigned validator commands can still fail for unrelated repo-wide drift. The skill should explicitly bless narrower feature-focused confirmation after the required command surfaces unrelated blockers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback includes a suggested skill change after the required test command timed out and exposed unrelated tests/app.test.ts token/bootstrap failures outside exchange-volume scope.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, runtime-provider-worker instructions, and commit 695feb7de7259cf3cc583b9f5f57ba6ffd81b035. I found no high-confidence blocking defects in the exchange-volume implementation; the feature appears to satisfy the volume-chart contract and live-ownership expectations, with only skill/procedure documentation gaps noted.
